title: Migrate pollinations.ai website to the Myceli Cloudflare account
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Moves the last piece of the stack — the website worker — into the Myceli Cloudflare account, matching enter/gen/media. The public URL stays `pollinations.ai`: the existing proxy in the old account forwards the apex to `pollinations.myceli.ai`, so there's no DNS move.

- Repoints the website worker + its deploy workflow at the Myceli account (`pollinations.myceli.ai` / `staging.pollinations.myceli.ai`)
- Proxy now forwards `pollinations.ai` → `pollinations.myceli.ai` (and staging)
- Removes the unused `/api/*` proxy + `PLN_APPS_KEY` — the frontend already calls the public APIs directly with the publishable BYOP key
- Aligns build/deploy scripts to the `@cloudflare/vite-plugin` pattern used by enter

Merging deploys the worker to Myceli (additive, zero-downtime); the apex flip to `pollinations.ai` is a deliberate follow-up step — deploy the proxy in the old account to reassign the custom domain.

Pre-existing SEO issue surfaced during this work, tracked separately: #11466

gist.headline: Moving day for the hive
gist.blurb: We've moved the main website worker over to our new Myceli Cloudflare account. Everything looks exactly the same on the outside, but it's much tidier on the inside.
gist.summary: Migrated the pollinations.ai website worker to the Myceli Cloudflare account, removing the unused /api/* proxy and aligning deploy scripts with the @cloudflare/vite-plugin pattern.
gist.impact: The public URL remains unchanged via a zero-downtime proxy, while the internal infrastructure is now fully consolidated under a single Cloudflare account.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Monitor Robot carrying a small glowing server box (representing the website worker) from an old garden bed to a fresh, new lime green garden bed labeled 'Myceli'.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny moving hat and a chunky Monitor Robot carrying a glowing server box to a fresh new garden bed. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
